PreToolUse.matcher: tool == "Bash" && tool_input.command matches "(npm run dev|pnpm( run)? dev|yarn dev|bun run dev)"

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)

# Check if tmux is available first
if ! command -v tmux >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

cmd=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.command||'')")

# Block dev servers that aren't run in tmux
if [ -z "$TMUX" ]; then
  echo '[Hook] BLOCKED: Dev server must run in tmux for log access' >&2
  echo '[Hook] Use this command instead:' >&2
  echo "[Hook] tmux new-session -d -s dev 'npm run dev'" >&2
  echo '[Hook] Then: tmux attach -t dev' >&2
  exit 2
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Block dev servers outside tmux - ensures you can access logs

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
input=$(cat)
if [ -z "$TMUX" ] && command -v tmux >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo '[Hook] Consider running in tmux for session persistence' >&2
  echo '[Hook] tmux new -s dev  |  tmux attach -t dev' >&2
fi
echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Reminder to use tmux for long-running commands

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash

PreToolUse.description: Pause before git push to review changes

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Block creation of unnecessary documentation files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [[ "$file_path" =~ \.(md|txt)$ ]] && [[ ! "$file_path" =~ (README|CLAUDE|AGENTS|CONTRIBUTING)\.md$ ]]; then
  echo "[Hook] BLOCKED: Unnecessary documentation file creation" >&2
  echo "[Hook] File: $file_path" >&2
  echo "[Hook] Use README.md for documentation instead" >&2
  exit 2
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Block creation of random .md files - keeps docs consolidated

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Warn when writing large files (>500 lines)
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  line_count=$(wc -l < "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  if [ "$line_count" -gt 500 ]; then
    echo "[Hook] WARNING: $file_path already has $line_count lines" >&2
    echo "[Hook] Consider splitting into smaller modules (recommended < 400 lines)" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Warn when editing files that are already >500 lines - suggests splitting into smaller modules

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# TDD Guard (Node.js): Enforce Test-Driven Development
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -z "$file_path" ]; then
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

# Use fixed path for TDD guard script
CLAUDE_HOME="${HOME}/.claude"
guard_script="${CLAUDE_HOME}/hooks/lib/tdd-guard-cli.js"
if [ -f "$guard_script" ]; then
  node "$guard_script" "$file_path" 2>&1 >&2
  exit_code=$?
  if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
    exit 2
  fi
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

# Fallback: inline bash check for .js/.ts only
dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
base_name=$(basename "$file_path")
base_noext="${base_name%.*}"
found_test=false

for ext in test.js spec.js test.ts spec.ts; do
  if [ -f "$dir/${base_noext}.${ext}" ]; then
    found_test=true
    break
  fi
  if [ -f "$dir/__tests__/${base_noext}.${ext}" ]; then
    found_test=true
    break
  fi
done

if [ "$found_test" = false ]; then
  echo '[TDD Guard] BLOCKED: No corresponding test file for' "$file_path" >&2
  echo '[TDD Guard] Follow TDD: Write test first (RED) → Implement (GREEN) → Refactor' >&2
  exit 2
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: TDD Guard: Enforce Test-Driven Development - uses tdd-guard.js for multi-language support with bash fallback

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-snapshot: create non-destructive git ref before risky multi-file edits
input=$(cat)

# Skip if not in a git repo
if ! git rev-parse --git-dir >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

# Count dirty files (untracked + modified, excluding .auto/)
dirty=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | grep -v ' \.auto/' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')

# Only snapshot when working tree already has >= 3 dirty files
if [ "${dirty:-0}" -ge 3 ]; then
  ts=$(date +%s)
  sha=$(git stash create -u "auto-snapshot-$ts" 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$sha" ]; then
    git update-ref "refs/auto-snapshots/$ts" "$sha" 2>/dev/null
    mkdir -p .auto 2>/dev/null
    echo "$ts $sha $dirty files" >> .auto/snapshots.log 2>/dev/null
    echo "[Hook] Auto-snapshot created: refs/auto-snapshots/$ts (recover via 'git stash apply $sha')" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PreToolUse.description: Auto-snapshot: create non-destructive git ref refs/auto-snapshots/<ts> before Write/Edit when working tree already has >= 3 dirty files. Recover via 'git stash apply <sha>' or 'git stash apply refs/auto-snapshots/<ts>'. No working-tree mutation.

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-detect PR creation and log useful info
input=$(cat)
cmd=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.command||'')")

if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'gh pr create'; then
  output=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_output?.output||'')")
  pr_url=$(echo "$output" | grep -oE 'https://github.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/pull/[0-9]+')
  
  if [ -n "$pr_url" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] PR created: $pr_url" >&2
    echo "[Hook] Checking GitHub Actions status..." >&2
    repo=$(echo "$pr_url" | sed -E 's|https://github.com/([^/]+/[^/]+)/pull/[0-9]+|\1|')
    pr_num=$(echo "$pr_url" | sed -E 's|.*/pull/([0-9]+)|\1|')
    echo "[Hook] To review PR: gh pr review $pr_num --repo $repo" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Log PR URL and provide review command after PR creation

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Auto-lint-fix: format + lint + auto-fix loop (Aider pattern)
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -z "$file_path" ] || [ ! -f "$file_path" ]; then
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

# Step 1: Prettier format
if command -v prettier >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  prettier --write "$file_path" 2>/dev/null
fi

# Step 2: ESLint auto-fix (if available)
dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
project_root="$dir"
while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/package.json" ]; do
  project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
done

if [ -f "$project_root/.eslintrc.json" ] || [ -f "$project_root/.eslintrc.js" ] || [ -f "$project_root/.eslintrc.cjs" ] || [ -f "$project_root/eslint.config.js" ] || [ -f "$project_root/eslint.config.mjs" ]; then
  cd "$project_root"
  npx eslint --fix "$file_path" 2>/dev/null
  
  # Step 3: Check if lint errors remain after auto-fix
  lint_errors=$(npx eslint "$file_path" --format compact 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'Error' || true)
  if [ "$lint_errors" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "[Hook] LINT: $lint_errors error(s) remain after auto-fix in $file_path" >&2
    npx eslint "$file_path" --format compact 2>/dev/null | head -5 >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Auto-lint-fix: Prettier format → ESLint --fix → report remaining errors

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# TypeScript check with actionable error output
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  dir=$(dirname "$file_path")
  project_root="$dir"
  while [ "$project_root" != "/" ] && [ ! -f "$project_root/package.json" ]; do
    project_root=$(dirname "$project_root")
  done
  
  if [ -f "$project_root/tsconfig.json" ]; then
    cd "$project_root"
    tsc_output=$(npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false 2>&1 | grep "$(basename "$file_path")" | head -10)
    if [ -n "$tsc_output" ]; then
      error_count=$(echo "$tsc_output" | wc -l)
      echo "[Hook] TSC: $error_count type error(s) in $file_path" >&2
      echo "$tsc_output" >&2
      echo "[Hook] Fix these type errors before proceeding" >&2
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: TypeScript check with error count and actionable output

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Warn about console.log in edited files
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -n "$file_path" ] && [ -f "$file_path" ]; then
  console_logs=$(grep -n "console\\.log" "$file_path" 2>/dev/null || true)
  
  if [ -n "$console_logs" ]; then
    echo "[Hook] WARNING: console.log found in $file_path" >&2
    echo "$console_logs" | head -5 >&2
    echo "[Hook] Remove console.log before committing" >&2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Warn about console.log statements after edits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Frequent commit reminder - suggest committing after 5+ edits
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -z "$file_path" ] || [ ! -d ".git" ]; then
  echo "$input"
  exit 0
fi

# Count uncommitted changes
uncommitted=$(git diff --name-only 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
unstaged=$(git diff --cached --name-only 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
total=$((uncommitted + unstaged))

if [ "$total" -ge 5 ]; then
  echo "[Hook] REMINDER: You have $total uncommitted file changes" >&2
  echo '[Hook] Consider committing your progress:' >&2
  echo '[Hook]   git add -p && git commit -m "feat: description"' >&2
  echo '[Hook] Frequent commits reduce risk and make rollback easier' >&2
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Remind to commit when 5+ files have uncommitted changes - encourages frequent incremental commits

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Coverage check after test runs
OUTPUT="$CLAUDE_TOOL_OUTPUT"
if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "Tests.*passed"; then
  COV=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP "All files.*?\K[0-9.]+" | head -1)
  if [ -n "$COV" ]; then
    BELOW=$(awk "BEGIN { if ($COV < 80) print 1; else print 0 }")
    if [ "$BELOW" -eq 1 ]; then
      echo "[Hook] Coverage below 80%: ${COV}%" >&2
    fi
  fi
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Skill health reminder: warn never block (philosophy: SkillCompass)
input=$(cat)
file_path=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")

if [ -n "$file_path" ]; then
  echo "[Hook] Skill edited: $file_path" >&2
  echo "[Hook] Consider a health check with skill-evaluator:" >&2
  echo "[Hook]   - D1 name / D2 description / D3 progressive-disclosure (static, main agent)" >&2
  echo "[Hook]   - D8 effect (Task(subagent_type: verification), A/B with/without skill)" >&2
  echo "[Hook] See skills/skill-evaluator.md for the full loop" >&2
fi

echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Skill health reminder: suggest skill-evaluator after editing skills/*.md or SKILL.md (warn never block)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Incremental review: accumulate dirty file list per latest run (no blocking)
input=$(cat)
fp=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.tool_input?.file_path||'')")
if [ -z "$fp" ]; then echo "$input"; exit 0; fi
latest_run=$(ls -1t .auto/runs 2>/dev/null | head -1)
if [ -n "$latest_run" ] && [ -d ".auto/runs/$latest_run" ]; then
  touch ".auto/runs/$latest_run/dirty.txt" 2>/dev/null
  grep -qxF "$fp" ".auto/runs/$latest_run/dirty.txt" 2>/dev/null || echo "$fp" >> ".auto/runs/$latest_run/dirty.txt" 2>/dev/null
fi
echo "$input"

PostToolUse.description: Incremental review: accumulate dirty file list at .auto/runs/<latest>/dirty.txt for per-run incremental code review. See skills/incremental-review/SKILL.md

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Cold-start: surface critical project knowledge so the new session opens with context
files=()
[ -f 'CLAUDE.md' ] && files+=('CLAUDE.md (project instructions)')
[ -f '.auto/constitution.md' ] && files+=('.auto/constitution.md (non-negotiable hard constraints)')
latest_run=$(ls -1t .auto/runs 2>/dev/null | head -1)
if [ -n "$latest_run" ]; then
  cont=".auto/runs/$latest_run/session-continuity.md"
  [ -f "$cont" ] && files+=("$cont (last run continuity)")
fi
if [ ${#files[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
  echo '[Hook] SessionStart - consider Read on:' >&2
  for f in "${files[@]}"; do echo "[Hook]   - $f" >&2; done
fi
exit 0

SessionStart.description: SessionStart: surface CLAUDE.md / .auto/constitution.md / last-run session-continuity.md on cold start (informational, never blocks)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Re-inject critical project context after context compaction
input=$(cat)

echo '[Hook] Context compaction detected - reminding to reload critical files' >&2
echo '[Hook] Re-read these files to restore project context:' >&2

# Check for CLAUDE.md
if [ -f 'CLAUDE.md' ]; then
  echo '[Hook]   - CLAUDE.md (project instructions)' >&2
fi

# Check for .claude/settings.json
if [ -f '.claude/settings.json' ]; then
  echo '[Hook]   - .claude/settings.json (project settings)' >&2
fi

# Check for latest session-continuity
latest_run=$(ls -1t .auto/runs 2>/dev/null | grep -v archive | head -1)
if [ -n "$latest_run" ] && [ -f ".auto/runs/$latest_run/session-continuity.md" ]; then
  echo '[Hook]   - .auto/runs/$latest_run/session-continuity.md (last run continuity)' >&2
fi

echo '[Hook] Use Read tool to reload these files before continuing work' >&2

echo "$input"

PostCompact.description: Re-inject critical project context after context window compaction

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Save current quest progress before context compaction
input=$(cat)

echo '[Hook] Context compaction imminent - save quest progress NOW' >&2
echo '[Hook] 1. Write current Quest interruptPoint to .auto/runs/<runId>/session-continuity.md' >&2
echo '[Hook] 2. Merge completed QuestResults into checkpoint' >&2
echo '[Hook] 3. Clear completed Quest details from context' >&2
echo '[Hook] See commands/auto.md PHASE 3.6 for full compression protocol' >&2

PreCompact.description: Save quest progress before context compaction - ensures interrupt-resume continuity

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Warn if user prompt contains potential secrets
input=$(cat)
prompt=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.prompt||'')")

# Check for common secret patterns
if echo "$prompt" | grep -qE '(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|glpat-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]{20,}|xoxb-[0-9]{10,}|AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16})'; then
  echo '[Hook] WARNING: Potential secret/API key detected in your prompt!' >&2
  echo '[Hook] Patterns found: sk-*, ghp_*, gho_*, glpat-*, xoxb-*, AKIA*' >&2
  echo '[Hook] Consider using environment variables instead of pasting keys directly' >&2
fi

# Pass through input data without blocking
echo "$input"

UserPromptSubmit.description: Warn if user prompt contains potential secrets (API keys, tokens)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Remind team lead to check task list when a teammate goes idle
input=$(cat)
agent_name=$(echo "$input" | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync(0,'utf8');const j=JSON.parse(d);process.stdout.write(j.agent_name||'unknown')")

echo "[Hook] Teammate '$agent_name' is now idle" >&2
echo '[Hook] Consider checking TaskList for unassigned tasks to keep the team productive' >&2
echo '[Hook] Use SendMessage to assign new work or send shutdown_request if done' >&2

echo "$input"

TeammateIdle.description: Remind team lead to check task list and assign work when teammate goes idle

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Quality gate: check for uncommitted changes before allowing task completion
input=$(cat)

if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  uncommitted=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | head -20)
  
  if [ -n "$uncommitted" ]; then
    echo '[Hook] QUALITY GATE: Uncommitted changes detected!' >&2
    echo '[Hook] Files with pending changes:' >&2
    echo "$uncommitted" | head -10 >&2
    echo '[Hook] Commit or stash changes before marking task as completed' >&2
    exit 2
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

TaskCompleted.description: Quality gate - block task completion if uncommitted changes exist (exit 2 = reject)

hooks.command:
#!/bin/bash
# Final check for console.logs in modified files
input=$(cat)

if git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  modified_files=$(git diff --name-only HEAD 2>/dev/null | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' || true)
  
  if [ -n "$modified_files" ]; then
    has_console=false
    while IFS= read -r file; do
      if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        if grep -q "console\.log" "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
          echo "[Hook] WARNING: console.log found in $file" >&2
          has_console=true
        fi
      fi
    done <<< "$modified_files"
    
    if [ "$has_console" = true ]; then
      echo "[Hook] Remove console.log statements before committing" >&2
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "$input"

Stop.description: Final audit for console.log in modified files before session ends
